AFTER THE OBLIGATORY PRAYERS

There are many Adhkaar that can be said after the obligatory prayer. From amon} them is:

1. To say: (ole S96) aol kes! I seek the forgiveness of Allah. (Three

O Allah, You are Peace and from You is all Peace.

3. To say: Jy Sh FG GLY city as Ya) Yo There is no deity worthy of worship, except Allah alone, having no partners. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise. He gives life and death and He is over all things, Omnipotent. To be said ten times after the Maghrib and

All of these praises should be counted on the hand. There is a difference of opinion about the authenticity of narration specifying the use of only the right hand, but there are general evidences that indicate the use of the right hand.'*?

O Allah, no one prevent what You have willed to bestow and no one can bestow what You have willed to prevent and no wealth or majesty can benefit anyone, as from You is all wealth and majesty.’

There is no deity worthy of worship, except Allah alone, having no partners. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise He is over all things, Omnipotent. There is no might, or power, except with Allah. There is no deity worthy of worship except Allah and We worship none, except Him. For Him is all favour, grace and glorious praise. There is no deity worthy of worship, except Allah. We are sincere in our faith to Him, even though the disbelievers dislike it.’** 6. To say:

‘My Lord, save me from Your punishment of the Day when You resurrect or gather Your slaves’.

This is from the following Hadeetht, narrated by Al-Barraah (4) who said: When we prayed behind the Messenger of Allah (%&), we wished to be on htis right so he would turn and face us after the prayer. And T heard him say,

the Day when You resurrect or gather

9 To recite the last three Suwar of the Qur'aan: CS! it AB “Say: ‘He is Allah the One, the Unique..." (Al-Ikhlaas 112:1 - 4) “Say: ‘I seek refuge with the Lord of the

cll She Set BD “Say: ‘I seek refuge with the Lord of mankind..."

Repeat each Sooralt three times, after the Fajr and Maghrib prayers. After all other obligatory prayers repeat them only once."

10. To Recite Aayah Al-Kursee:

Some of the benefits of observing and preserving these Sunan after every obligatory prayer:

Co If a Muslim preserves these supplications after every obligatory prayer then five hundred Sadagaat are written for him. from the saying of the Prophet (48):

Every Tasbeeh is Sadagah, every Takbeer |5 Sadagah, every Tahmeed is Sadagah and every Taheel is Sadagah.**'

Imaam An-Nawawee said a person gains

the reward as if he has paid this amou in charity.

' The narrations that mention to supplicate in the place of prayer, refer to after Salaah Al-Fajr and Al-Asr only, and are therefore not applicable other prayers. Readers should note that narration was found to support this point.

Q Five hundred trees will be planted for the Muslim who preserves these supplication after every prayer during the day and night:

The Messenger of Allah (3%) passed by Aboo Hurayrah () while he was planting something and said:

‘O Aboo Hurayrali, shall I not show you a planting better than this?’

He said:‘Of course, O Messenger of Allah.’

He () said: ‘say:...

-.subhaan allaal, al-hamdu lillaah, laa

ilgaha illallaah, allaahu akbar...

and a tree will be planted for you in

Paradise for every one you say’.!?

0 For the one who preserves the reading of Aayah Al-Kursee after every Salaah, there is nothing between him and entering Paradise, except death.’**

O Whoever preserves these praises will have his sins removed, even if they were

as abundant as the foam on the sea.!*

The one who preserves these praises after every prayer, will not be afflicted with failure or disgrace, in this world or the Hereafter:

C It compensates for the lacking and

deficiency in one’s performance of the obligatory prayers."

ADHKAAR OF THE MORNING AND EVENING

Some Adiikaar to be said in the morning and evening: 1. Aayah Al-Kursee: €.. ee call Sa ¥y Sy Yat “Allah, none has the right to be worshipped, but He, the Ever-Living...”" (Al-Bagarah, 2:255) From its benefits is:

Whoever says it in the morning will be protected from the jinn until evening, and whoever says it in the evening will be protected from them until the morning."*”

“Say:'He is Allah the One, the Unique...””

4G 25. Set ip “Say:'I seek refuge with the Lord of daybreak...”

KB 55, Sel SD

“Say: ‘I seek refuge with the Lord of mankind...’

From its benefits is that: Whoever says them three times in the evening and morning, they will be sufficient for him in everything"!

In Sunan ibn Maajah:

and actions that are acceptable. (To be said in the morning)"

In Sunan At-Tirmidhee and others:

I seek refuge in the perfect Words of Allah, from every evil created. (Three times in the evening)”

In Sunan Aboo Daawood:

O Allah, verily, I have reached the morning and I call on You, the bearers of Your throne, Your angels and all of Your

creation witness that You are Allah,

None has the right to be worshipped, except You, alone, having no partner and that Muhammad (8s) is Your servant and Messenger. (Four times)">*

From its benefits, is that whoever says it in the morning or evening four times, Allah will free him from the Fire.

in the nme of Sieh fy aah

9. In Sunan Aboo Daawood and Musnad Imaam Ahmad:

Z alk a4 wrae A ale eb YAY agai o Be HU et

(ol SK) er O Allah, grant my body health.

O Allah, grant my hearing health.

(ole BW) AE) O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief and poverty.

O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave.

No one has the right to be worshipped, except You. (Three times)

Both of the above supplication come in the same narration. They have been Separated because in the hadith they did not come as one single supplication. They are to be read three times each in the morning and evening.

O Allah, You are my Lord: none has the right to be worshipped, except You. You created me and I am Your servant and I abide by Your covenant and promise as best as I can.
